pr.title: ft: Match ftKirby and ftPp special functions (part 2)
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Matched a small batch of Kirby and Popo fighter special/init callbacks, replacing several placeholder declarations with simple C implementations and tightening related prototypes. The PR body states 100% matches for ftKb_Init_UnkCallbackPairs0_1, ftPp_SpecialHiStart_0_Coll, ftPp_SpecialHiThrow_0_Coll, ftPp_SpecialHi_80122098, and ftPp_SpecialHiThrow_1_Anim. It also corrected callback/function signatures in ftKb_Init.h and ftanim.h, notably giving ftAnim_80070458 a concrete Kirby animation-related signature.
what_changed: Implemented ftKb_Init_UnkCallbackPairs0_1, checking Kirby hat state via fp->fv.kb.hat.x14.data and fp->fv.kb.hat.jobj before calling ftAnim_80070458(fp, &fp->fv.kb.x44, arg1).

Changed ftKb_Init_OnItemInvisible from a generic HSD_GObj signature and Fighter_OnItemInvisible wrapper call to a Fighter_GObj implementation that checks it_8026B2B4(fp->item_gobj) and conditionally calls ftAnim_80070CC4(gobj, 1).
Implemented ftPp_SpecialHiStart_0_Coll as a ground-collision check: if ft_800827A0(gobj) returns 0, it transitions through ftPp_SpecialHi_801217EC(gobj).
Implemented ftPp_SpecialHiThrow_0_Coll with the same ft_800827A0(gobj) == 0 collision pattern, transitioning through ftPp_SpecialHi_80121CE0(gobj).
Implemented ftPp_SpecialHi_80122098 by preserving fp->cur_anim_frame while calling Fighter_ChangeMotionState(gobj, 0x15E, 0, fp->cur_anim_frame, 1.0f, 0.0f, NULL).
Implemented ftPp_SpecialHiThrow_1_Anim to end the throw animation by calling ft_8008A2BC(gobj) when ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining(gobj) returns 0.
Added multiple ftPp_SpecialS.h prototypes for Popo SpecialHi start/throw IASA, Coll, transition, and animation helpers.

Refined header prototypes: ftKb_Init_UnkDemoCallbacks0 now names parameters kind, out1, and out2; ftKb_SpecialN_800F1420 now takes a u32* arg1; ftAnim_80070458 changed from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void ftAnim_80070458(Fighter*, struct KirbyFV_x44_t*, int).
smart_moves: Used existing fighter helper idioms rather than open-coding state transitions: ft_800827A0 for ground/collision checks, Fighter_ChangeMotionState for action-state transitions, ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining for animation completion, and ft_8008A2BC for post-animation cleanup/transition.

The ftAnim_80070458 prototype was made concrete only once call-site evidence identified its arguments: Fighter*, KirbyFV_x44_t*, and int. This improves type safety for future Kirby hat animation work.
The Popo collision callbacks share a minimal matching pattern, each dispatching to the appropriate transition helper only when ft_800827A0 reports the expected condition.
The Kirby hat callback guards both data availability and JObj absence before invoking ftAnim_80070458, which documents a useful invariant for similar Kirby hat-related callbacks.
Header additions in ftPp_SpecialS.h expose newly matched local helpers by address and name, improving cross-file reuse and future matching context.
decomp_lessons: Small fighter callbacks often match as thin wrappers around established engine helpers; looking for repeated patterns in neighboring action callbacks can reveal the correct control flow.
For collision callbacks, a common form is `if (ft_800827A0(gobj) == 0) { transition(gobj); }`; this pattern should be checked when matching similar grounded special-state collision functions.
For animation callbacks, `if (ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining(gobj) == 0)` is a strong signal for end-of-animation transition logic.
Kirby hat/init code may require inspecting nested fighter-vars such as `fp->fv.kb.hat.x14.data`, `fp->fv.kb.hat.jobj`, and `fp->fv.kb.x44` to recover correct calls into ftanim helpers.
Replacing UNK_PARAMS prototypes with concrete signatures can be part of matching a function, especially when a newly matched call site provides enough argument evidence.
Preserving `fp->cur_anim_frame` in Fighter_ChangeMotionState calls can be necessary for matching mid-animation action-state transitions.
naming_conventions: Fighter callback names follow character/module/action/phase suffixes such as ftPp_SpecialHiStart_0_Coll, ftPp_SpecialHiThrow_1_Anim, and ftKb_Init_UnkCallbackPairs0_1.
Address-suffixed unknown helpers remain in use when behavior is not semantically named, e.g. ftPp_SpecialHi_80122098, ftPp_SpecialHi_801217EC, and ftKb_SpecialN_800F1420.
Header comments retain hex address tags such as /* 122098 */ next to function declarations.
Newly clarified prototypes should prefer meaningful parameter names where known, as shown by ftKb_Init_UnkDemoCallbacks0(int kind, int* out1, int* out2).
Use Fighter_GObj* for fighter object callbacks when the function immediately treats the gobj as a fighter object.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Convert placeholder `/// #function` stubs into minimal control-flow wrappers around known helpers when the assembly pattern is a simple conditional branch and call.
Use return-value comparisons exactly, e.g. `ft_800827A0(gobj) == 0` and `ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining(gobj) == 0`, to match branch structure.
For Fighter_ChangeMotionState matches, preserve literal action-state IDs and float constants; this PR used state `0x15E`, flags `0`, current frame, rate `1.0f`, blend/start value `0.0f`, and NULL.
Infer missing function prototypes from successful call sites, as with ftAnim_80070458(Fighter*, struct KirbyFV_x44_t*, int).
Use `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` once and access fighter vars through the resulting `Fighter* fp` to match common fighter-source style and expected codegen.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The only human text excerpt is the PR summary listing five functions matched at 100%; no reviewer rationale or requested changes were available.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching ftAnim_80070458 to see whether the KirbyFV_x44_t signature was reused or further refined.
Search for other ftPp_SpecialHi collision callbacks using ft_800827A0 to propagate the same matching pattern.
Search for Kirby hat callbacks that check fp->fv.kb.hat.x14.data and fp->fv.kb.hat.jobj before animation setup.
Review surrounding ftPp_SpecialHi_801217EC and ftPp_SpecialHi_80121CE0 implementations for opportunities to name the transition helpers semantically.
Check subsequent ftKb_SpecialN_800F1420 work for confirmation of the u32* arg1 prototype.
